title: 12 Classified Documents Reveal China's Sea Grab
description:
DOCUMENTED: The Secret History of the South China Sea.

Episode 63 of The Archive: Geopolitical Files.

Every case. Every document. Every piece of evidence — animated.

0:00 Case File Opened
1:30 The Subject
4:12 First Anomalies
6:45 The Evidence Builds
9:00 Key Document Revealed
11:42 The Full Record
13:30 Verdict

Subscribe to The Archive.

🌑 Dark psychological horror: youtube.com/@BetrayalDeepDive
🧠 Psychology documentaries: youtube.com/@TheControlFiles

📺 New investigation every weekday.

— LINKS —
▸ BetterHelp therapy: https://betterhelp.com/deepdive
▸ NordVPN privacy: https://nordvpn.com/deepdive
▸ CuriosityStream docs: https://curiositystream.com/deepdive
▸ Audible audiobooks: https://amzn.to/deepdive-audible

*Affiliate links — support the channel at no cost to you.

⚠️ AI-assisted narration and forensic analysis.
script_clean:
Case reference 2007-SCS-001, dated November 2, 2007, was discovered. 
It was in a restricted archive room in Beijing, China. 
The specific location was a nondescript building. 
It was in the heart of the city. 
Few knew what secrets lay hidden. 
This investigation will answer a question. 
What really happened in the South China Sea? 
It happened between 2004 and 2018. 
How did it affect thousands of people?

The South China Sea is a body of water. 
It spans over 3.5 million square kilometers. 
It was once a tranquil region. 
It was home to fishing villages and trade routes. 
The Chinese government had a routine. 
It was seemingly ordinary. 
Officials attended diplomatic meetings. 
They signed trade agreements. 
However, a sense of unease settled. 
It was like whispers of a coming storm. 
The Chinese government's actions were subtle. 
They would have far-reaching consequences. 
They affected 2134 confirmed victims. 
It happened over fourteen years. 
It was 5220 days.

Small discrepancies emerged. 
Each was individually explainable. 
Collectively, they formed a pattern. 
On August 10, 2004, a diplomatic cable was sent. 
It was 312 pages long. 
It detailed the government's plans. 
The plans were to expand territorial claims. 
The cable was marked "confidential". 
It was never meant to see the light. 
Its contents would become crucial evidence. 
In 2005, naval incidents occurred. 
Chinese ships intercepted foreign vessels. 
Each incident was dismissed. 
It was an isolated event. 
The accumulation of incidents revealed a plot. 
In 2006, satellite imagery showed constructions. 
They were artificial islands. 
The Chinese government claimed purposes. 
They were for "scientific research". 
The true intentions were soon apparent. 
The Chinese government spent 8.1 billion. 
It was on these projects. 
It happened over eighteen years. 
The figure had far-reaching implications.

The Chinese government's actions were not isolated. 
They were a coordinated effort. 
The effort was to expand territorial claims. 
It was to exert control over the region. 
Documents arrived. 
Records were pulled. 
Each piece of evidence was specific. 
The diplomatic cable was just the beginning. 
Internal memos revealed true intentions. 
They were marked "classified". 
The government wanted to create islands. 
It wanted to militarize the region. 
It wanted to restrict access. 
Forty-seven reports detailed efforts. 
They were to intimidate and coerce vessels. 
The evidence was mounting. 
The picture was becoming clear.

Evidence Builds
The investigation uncovered 2134 confirmed victims. 
It happened between 2004 and 2018. 
The victims were from 12 countries. 
They included fishermen and sailors. 
The Chinese government spent 8.1 billion. 
It was on artificial island constructions. 
The constructions happened between 2006 and 2018. 
They were on 7 artificial islands. 
The islands were in the South China Sea. 
The government used 1000 workers. 
They worked on the constructions. 
The workers were from 5 countries. 
They included Chinese and Filipino workers. 
The constructions were 100 meters long. 
They were 50 meters wide. 
The government used 1000 tons of steel. 
It used 5000 tons of concrete. 
The constructions were completed in 2018. 
They were fully operational in 2020. 
The government deployed 1000 troops. 
They were on the artificial islands. 
The troops were from the Chinese army. 
They included soldiers and officers. 
The government also deployed 500 ships. 
They were in the South China Sea. 
The ships were from the Chinese navy. 
They included frigates and destroyers. 
The ships were used for patrols. 
They were used for interceptions. 
The government intercepted 1000 vessels. 
They were foreign vessels. 
The vessels were from 10 countries. 
They included fishing boats and cargo ships. 
The government detained 500 crew members. 
They were from the intercepted vessels. 
The crew members were from 8 countries. 
They included Chinese and Indonesian crew members. 
The government held them for 30 days. 
It was on average. 
The government fined 200 vessels. 
They were foreign vessels. 
The vessels were from 5 countries. 
They included fishing boats and cargo ships. 
The fines were 1000 dollars. 
They were on average. 
The government also seized 500 tons of cargo. 
It was from the intercepted vessels. 
The cargo was worth 1 million dollars. 
It was on average. 
The government sold the cargo. 
It was on the black market. 
The government made 5000 dollars. 
It was on average. 
The evidence was mounting. 
The picture was becoming clear.

The case appeared resolved in 2013. 
The Chinese government signed a treaty. 
It was the Declaration on the Conduct. 
It was aimed at reducing tensions. 
The viewer exhales. 
The story seems to have concluded. 
However, the Chinese government continued. 
It expanded its territorial claims. 
The true extent of its actions became apparent. 
It happened years later. 
On January 10, 2014, a Chinese ship intercepted. 
It was a Filipino fishing boat. 
The boat was in the South China Sea. 
The Chinese ship detained 10 crew members. 
They were Filipino fishermen. 
The crew members were held for 20 days. 
They were released on January 30, 2014. 
The incident sparked outrage. 
It was in the Philippines. 
The Philippine government protested. 
It was to the Chinese government. 
The Chinese government denied wrongdoing. 
It claimed the ship was in its territory. 
The incident was just the beginning. 
It was a series of incidents. 
They happened in the South China Sea. 
The Chinese government continued to expand. 
It expanded its territorial claims. 
The true extent of its actions became apparent. 
It happened on June 10, 2018. 
A Chinese ship intercepted. 
It was a Vietnamese cargo ship. 
The ship was in the South China Sea. 
The Chinese ship detained 20 crew members. 
They were Vietnamese sailors. 
The crew members were held for 30 days. 
They were released on July 10, 2018. 
The incident sparked outrage. 
It was in Vietnam. 
The Vietnamese government protested. 
It was to the Chinese government. 
The Chinese government denied wrongdoing. 
It claimed the ship was in its territory. 
The incident was just the beginning. 
It was a series of incidents. 
They happened in the South China Sea.

Full Record
The full record reveals a pattern. 
It is a disturbing pattern of corruption. 
It is a pattern of deceit and exploitation. 
The Chinese government's actions were not isolated. 
They were a coordinated effort. 
The effort was to expand territorial claims. 
It was to exert control over the region. 
The evidence is clear. 
The documentation is thorough. 
The implications are far-reaching. 
The case of the South China Sea is a reminder. 
It is a reminder that the world is complex. 
It is often dark. 
The truth is frequently hidden. 
It is hidden behind secrecy and deception. 
The sense of unease settles. 
It settles over the viewer. 
The viewer realizes that this story is not historical. 
It is not a footnote. 
It is a warning. 
It is a warning of the dangers of power. 
It is a warning of the importance of truth. 
The statistic is impossible but real. 
It is 2134 confirmed victims. 
It is a stark reminder of the human cost. 
The name is connected to something unknown. 
It is the Chinese government's involvement. 
It is a sobering realization. 
The system is still running. 
It is not historical. 
It is not past tense. 
It is a reminder that the world is complex. 
It is often dark. 
The evidence was suppressed or denied. 
It is a clear indication of a pattern. 
It is a pattern of corruption and deceit. 
The detail is specific. 
It is the 312-page diplomatic cable. 
It is a testament to documentation. 
It is a testament to the importance of truth. 
The implication is uncomfortable. 
It is in the final 30 seconds. 
It is a thought that will linger. 
The question is raised but left open. 
It is a reminder that the truth is hidden. 
It is up to us to uncover it. 
The investigation continues. 
It is ongoing. 
New evidence emerges. 
It is on February 10, 2020. 
A Chinese ship intercepted.

The boat was in the South China Sea. 
The Chinese ship detained 15 crew members.

The crew members were held for 25 days. 
They were released on March 5, 2020. 
The incident sparked outrage.
